anti_patterns.description: Champion-3 OP-001 and debate summary projected 4.0-5.3% E2E improvement from 1.063x cold-cache kernel speedup. OP-001v2 (which fixed the torch.compile integration failure) measured only 0.82% mean E2E improvement in a same-session paired A/B sweep. The ratio 5.3/0.82 = 6.5x far exceeds the 2x threshold. The overestimation arose from projecting all 7 GEMM shapes would use Triton; in production the highest-frequency shape (K=2560, N=12288, 24 calls/step) consistently fell back to CUTLASS.

citations.claim: Debate judge accepted 4.0-5.3% E2E projection for OP-001 based solely on cold-cache 1.063x kernel speedup
citations.quoted_content:
- **Measured kernel speedup**: 1.063x (cold-cache, all 7 shapes improve, CUDA graph captured)
- **Expected E2E**: 4.0-5.3% improvement
- **Implementation**: ~230 LOC new Triton code, ~40 LOC integration

citations.claim: Actual measured E2E improvement in paired A/B sweep: 0.82% mean, 6.5x below the 5.3% projected
Primary GEMM shape (24 calls/step, largest contributor) fell back to CUTLASS, explaining the gap
citations.quoted_content:
| GDN in_proj_qkvz | 8 | 2560 | 12288 | 24 | 130.7 | 132.1 | **CUTLASS (fallback)** |
...
**Critical observation**: The single most-called M=8 shape (K=2560, N=12288 -- GDN in_proj_qkvz, 24 instances/step) falls back to CUTLASS during production dispatch.

debate_quality_evidence.description: Round 1 debate selected OP-001 (Champion-3 Triton GEMM, 7.50) over Champion-1 (5.25) via same-component tiebreak. Champion-2 (GDN fused, 7.65) selected as orthogonal complement. The same-component rule forced one GEMM champion to be eliminated despite both having micro-experiment data.

citations.claim: Champion-1 scored 5.25, Champion-2 scored 7.65, Champion-3 scored 7.50; two GEMM champions competing, tiebreak eliminates lower scorer
citations.quoted_content:
| Custom kernel gate | PASS | PASS | PASS |
| Proposal quality (15%) | 7/10 | 8/10 | 9/10 |
| Feasibility evidence (25%) | 6/10 | 9/10 | 8/10 |
| E2E impact potential (20%) | 5/10 | 4/10 | 8/10 |
| Survived critiques (25%) | 4/10 | 8/10 | 7/10 |
| Implementation complexity (10%) | 7/10 | 9/10 | 8/10 |
| Complementarity (5%) | 0/10 | 10/10 | 0/10 |
| **Weighted Total** | **5.25** | **7.65** | **7.50** |

debate_quality_evidence.description: Debate filtering scored as partially incorrect (0.5/2 pts). Debate accepted OP-001 with 4.0-5.3% E2E projection that proved to be a 6.5x overestimate. Combined projected E2E was ~6.0% but actual total shipped was 1.82%.

citations.claim: Combined projected E2E impact was ~6.0% (GEMM 5.3% + GDN 0.75%) vs actual 1.82% cumulative
citations.quoted_content: If both optimizations ship: ~5.3% (GEMM) + ~0.75% (GDN) = **~6.0% total E2E improvement** (multiplicative: 1.053 × 1.0075 = 1.061x)

debate_quality_evidence.description: Round 2 debate correctly identified all candidates were below the 3% diminishing returns threshold individually. OP-003 scored 5.95 (GDN inter-GEMM fusion, ~1.55% f_decode), OP-004 scored 5.60 (flash-decode hd=256, ~1.30% f_decode).

citations.claim: Round 2 noted all candidates below 3% threshold; OP-003 and OP-004 queued to pending_queue
citations.quoted_content:
**All candidates are below the 3% diminishing returns threshold individually.** No candidate can deliver meaningful standalone E2E improvement.

### Queued for pending_queue (conditional on re-profiling):

1. **OP-003: GDN Inter-GEMM Fusion** (Champion-1, 5.95) — Targets ~1.55% f_decode across launch-overhead-dominated micro-kernels.

bottleneck_profile.model_architecture_notes: Qwen3.5-4B uses GDN (Gated Delta Network) with 24 linear attention layers + 8 full attention layers. Decode is M=8 BW-bound. 585 kernel launches/step.

citations.claim: GEMM dominates at 89.0% of GPU time; GDN recurrent at 4.9%; FlashAttention at 1.2%
citations.quoted_content:
| **GEMM (all CUTLASS + cuBLAS)** | **89.0%** | **13,315** | **34,087** |
| GDN Recurrent | 4.9% | 737 | 1,886 |
| FlashAttention (incl. combine) | 1.2% | 183 | 468 |

citations.claim: Measured BW utilization 69.5% with 1.44x physical ceiling; 8.0 GB GEMM weight per decode step
citations.quoted_content:
| Total GEMM weight bytes per decode step | ~8.0 GB |
| BW-bound floor (864 GB/s) | 9,260 us |
| Observed GEMM per decode step | 13,315 us |
| **Measured BW utilization** | **69.5%** |
| Physical speedup ceiling (at 100% BW) | 1.44x |

hallucinated_data.description: bottleneck_analysis.md Section 2 lists GDN in_proj_ba as K=2560, N=8192, Weight=40 MB. Actual production shape is K=2560, N=64 (weight=320 KB). Error is 128x in N dimension. Propagated to Champion-1 and Champion-3 proposals.

citations.claim: bottleneck_analysis.md incorrectly lists GDN in_proj_ba with N=8192, K=2560, Weight=40 MB
Delegate-1a discovered actual shape K=2560, N=64 from source code; bottleneck_analysis shape wrong by 128x in N
citations.quoted_content:
**The bottleneck_analysis.md has an incorrect shape for in_proj_ba.**

From source code `qwen3_next.py:415-420`:
```python
self.in_proj_ba = MergedColumnParallelLinear(
    input_size=self.hidden_size,       # K = 2560
    output_sizes=[self.num_v_heads] * 2,  # [32, 32] → total N = 64

hallucinated_data.description: Champion-3 proposal reports 1.037x cold-cache speedup for 'GDN in_proj_ba (K=2560, N=8192)' -- a kernel shape that does not exist in production decode. Contributed to the weighted 1.063x aggregate speedup.

citations.claim: Champion-3 reported 1.037x speedup for GDN in_proj_ba at N=8192 -- a production shape that does not exist

hallucinated_data.description: Champion-1 proposal lists GDN in_proj_ba K=2560, N=8192 with 1.049x speedup from same non-existent shape. Corrected in argument via delegate-1a but proposal micro-experiment data was never re-run.

citations.claim: Champion-1 reported 1.049x speedup for GDN in_proj_ba at N=8192 in proposal micro-experiment

wasted_retries.description: OP-001 E2E sweep run with --labels opt only, leaving no valid paired baseline. The baseline run failed (returncode=1).

citations.claim: OP-001 baseline E2E run failed; sweep was opt-only, no paired comparison
citations.quoted_content:
### Failure 2: No valid paired baseline measurement

The E2E sweep was run with `--labels opt` (optimized only). The baseline run in the sweep **failed** (`returncode=1`, `ok=null`, `metrics={}`).

wasted_retries.description: OP-003 required 4 separate E2E measurement runs to resolve an Amdahl violation. Gate 5.2 isolated benchmarks underestimated production component share (f=0.88% isolated vs f=1.35% nsys production).

citations.claim: Amdahl violation triggered: 0.92% measured vs 0.59% expected max; ratio 2.33x exceeds 1.5x threshold
citations.quoted_content: **FLAG: Amdahl violation: claimed 0.92% but expected max 0.59% (Gate 5.2: f_split=0.00880 s=1.71x, f_norm=0.00530 s=1.06x; expected_e2e=0.395%; 0.395%x1.5=0.593%). Possible cross-track contamination or measurement error. Investigate before proceeding.**

citations.claim: Root cause: isolated benchmark underestimates production copy kernel time by 2.2x

wasted_retries.description: OP-004 ran E2E sweep with broken dispatch before the CUDA graph constexpr freeze bug was identified.

citations.claim: OP-004 failed with CUDA graph constexpr bug; weighted kernel speedup 0.90x (regression)
citations.quoted_content:
"OP-004": {
  "status": "fail",
  "correctness": "fail",
  "kernel_speedup": 0.9,
  "e2e_note": "FAIL: CUDA graph constexpr bug (MAX_NUM_KV_ITERS=1 during capture). Weighted kernel speedup 0.90x (regression). Even with bug fix, kernel too slow."

off_track_reasoning.description: Champion-2 Round 2 (OP-004 flash-decode for head_dim=256) projected 1.48x component speedup without a working prototype kernel. The implemented kernel achieved only 0.90x weighted speedup (10% regression) and failed correctness under CUDA graphs.

citations.claim: Debate summary selected OP-004 based on 1.30% f_decode target with no prototype kernel speedup measurement
citations.quoted_content: 2. **OP-004: Custom Flash-Decode hd=256** (Champion-2, 5.60) — Targets ~1.30% f_decode. Technically interesting (single-pass softmax, fused output gate). Higher complexity.

citations.claim: Kernel regressed at medium-long sequences: weighted speedup 0.90x, not the projected 1.48x

track_outcomes.technique: Triton autotuned GEMM for M=8 decode
track_outcomes.failure_reason: torch.compile bypasses dispatch point; Triton GEMM never executes for target decode shapes under production parity

citations.claim: Gate 5.3 FAIL: torch.compile with custom_ops: ['none'] traces through F.linear, bypassing dispatch_unquantized_gemm
citations.quoted_content: **Root cause**: vLLM uses `torch.compile` with `custom_ops: ['none']` (production default). This means torch.compile traces through ALL custom ops, including `torch.nn.functional.linear`. The inductor compiler fuses the linear operations into compiled CUDA kernels, bypassing `default_unquantized_gemm()` entirely

track_outcomes.technique: BV=8 + gating fusion in Triton GDN kernel

citations.claim: Same-session paired A/B: baseline 7.737s, opt 7.664s, 0.94% improvement; Amdahl ratio 0.81x

track_outcomes.technique: Fused GDN pre-recurrent + post-recurrent Triton kernels

citations.claim: Paired A/B v3: baseline 7.739s, opt 7.668s, 0.92% improvement; reproduced in v4 (fresh cache) at 0.85%

track_outcomes.technique: Custom Triton flash-decoding for head_dim=256 + fused output gate
track_outcomes.failure_reason: CUDA graph constexpr bug (MAX_NUM_KV_ITERS=1 during capture) + weighted kernel regression (0.90x)

citations.claim: CUDA graph constexpr freeze: MAX_NUM_KV_ITERS frozen at 1, kernel only reads first 64 tokens
citations.quoted_content: The Triton kernel parameter `MAX_NUM_KV_ITERS` is a `tl.constexpr` (compile-time constant). During CUDA graph capture, `attn_metadata.max_seq_len = 1` (capture uses dummy metadata), so `MAX_NUM_KV_ITERS = ceil(1/64) = 1`.

track_outcomes.technique: Triton GEMM via torch.compile-safe custom op dispatch
track_outcomes.failure_reason: Kernel not fast enough; primary shape K=2560,N=12288 falls back to CUTLASS; 0.82% mean below 2% threshold

citations.claim: Primary shape CUTLASS fallback: K=2560,N=12288 (24/step) probes slower for Triton
citations.quoted_content: | GDN in_proj_qkvz | 8 | 2560 | 12288 | 24 | 130.7 | 132.1 | **CUTLASS (fallback)** |

    output_sizes=[self.num_v_heads] * 2,  # [32, 32] → total N = 64

delegation_failures.description: Delegate-1a incorrectly claimed dispatch_unquantized_gemm hook 'bypasses torch.compile entirely'. In production, torch.compile traces through F.linear. Directly caused OP-001 Gate 5.3 failure.

citations.claim: Delegate-1a incorrectly stated dispatch hook bypasses torch.compile
citations.quoted_content: 1. **Default case (CUTLASS WMMA)**: A custom Triton GEMV/split-K kernel bypasses torch.compile entirely via `dispatch_unquantized_gemm()` hook — operates in both modes.

citations.claim: Production shows dispatch hook is never reached; torch.compile fuses through F.linear
citations.quoted_content: **Root cause**: vLLM uses `torch.compile` with `custom_ops: ['none']` (production default). This means torch.compile traces through ALL custom ops, including `torch.nn.functional.linear`.

delegation_efficiency_issues.description: Delegate-1a and delegate-3a both independently researched CUTLASS WMMA kernel identity, grid analysis, and per-shape roofline for the same decode GEMM target with same hardware constants.

citations.claim: Delegate-1a produced CUTLASS kernel trace and grid analysis, overlapping delegate-3a
citations.quoted_content:
### Task 2: Production GEMM Kernel Identity

### Authoritative Answer: CUTLASS WMMA (Confirmed from nsys CSV)

- Peak BF16 Compute: 366 TFLOPS
- **Breakeven AI = 366e12 / 864e9 = 423.6 FLOP/byte**

citations.claim: OP-001v2 mean 0.82% with P50 2.73% exceeding Amdahl ceiling -- unexplained

torch_compile_interactions.description: OP-001 FAIL: torch.compile with custom_ops: ['none'] traces through F.linear, bypassing GEMM dispatch hook entirely for decode shapes.

citations.claim: torch.compile fusion through F.linear prevents dispatch hook from executing
citations.quoted_content: vLLM uses `torch.compile` with `custom_ops: ['none']` (production default). This means torch.compile traces through ALL custom ops, including `torch.nn.functional.linear`.

torch_compile_interactions.description: OP-001v2 FIX: Registered cuda_unquantized_gemm as opaque custom op via direct_register_custom_op(). Integration fix works (38 shapes probed vs 0 for OP-001) but kernel not fast enough.

citations.claim: Opaque custom op registration makes GEMM dispatch survive torch.compile
citations.quoted_content: Register a `torch.ops.vllm.cuda_unquantized_gemm` custom op via `direct_register_custom_op()`. This makes the entire GEMM dispatch opaque to torch.compile

torch_compile_interactions.description: OP-003 SURPRISE: torch.compile graph restructuring produced non-deterministic kernel redistribution when copy kernels removed. Net 0.92% improvement includes inductor recompilation side effects.

citations.claim: torch.compile generated different Triton kernel compositions: 6 removed, 3 added, 5 increased
citations.quoted_content:
- 6 Triton kernels exist ONLY in baseline (306.72 ms total, removed)
- 3 Triton kernels exist ONLY in opt (121.63 ms total, added)
- Several common kernels show INCREASED time in opt

torch_compile_interactions.description: OP-004 FAIL: Triton constexpr parameter frozen at CUDA graph capture time with dummy metadata. Produces incorrect output for seq_len > 64.

citations.claim: Architectural mismatch: vLLM pipeline requires static shapes per captured graph, Triton constexpr freezes at capture time
citations.quoted_content: Both bugs stem from the same architectural mismatch: vLLM's production pipeline (torch.compile + CUDA graphs) requires kernels with **static shapes and fixed loop bounds** per captured graph.

skill_version_evidence.key_changes: Simplified campaign loop by merging campaign-loop.md into SKILL.md; merged Lead rules into SKILL.md; removed deprecated state.json fields (opportunity_attempts, status, verification_run); gate scoring changed from first-attempt pass rate to pure pass/fail; hook enforcement section added

citations.claim: Refactored skill version: simplified campaign loop, merged rules/ammo.md
citations.quoted_content:
"git_commit": "99a0fbed6",
  "description": "refactored skill by simplifying campaign loop, merge in rules/ammo.md",

gemm_intractability_evidence.description: GEMM (89% of decode time) proved intractable under torch.compile across all 3 attempts. OP-001 failed dispatch bypass. OP-001v2 fixed integration but primary shape falls back to CUTLASS. The 30.5% BW utilization gap remains unaddressed.

citations.claim: Campaign summary confirms GEMM intractability
citations.quoted_content: "summary": "Campaign EXHAUSTED (threshold disabled). Shipped: OP-002 (0.94%) + OP-003 (0.87%). Cumulative: 1.0182x (1.8%). GEMM (89%) remains intractable: torch.compile integration works but kernel not fast enough."

citations.claim: 30.5% BW gap root cause not diagnosed; would require ncu analysis
citations.quoted_content: The 30.5% BW gap is expected for small-M (M=8) GEMM: insufficient work to fully saturate memory bandwidth channels, plus tiling/padding overhead. The root cause of the gap has not been diagnosed -- ncu analysis would be needed

cold_to_production_translation_factors.notes: Using corrected nsys f_production=1.35%. Copy kernel overhead 2.2x higher in production vs isolated.
Not applicable -- primary shape falls back to CUTLASS. Effective kernel speedup ~1.01x, not 1.025x.
